paper_focus.title: Promoting strength-ductility synergy by heterogeneous grain structure in a zirconium-alloyed Fe-Mn-Al-C low-density steel
paper_focus.research_objective: To design a single-phase heterogeneous grain structure in Fe-Mn-Al-C low-density steel that mitigates strain localization and hetero-interfacial cracking, thereby achieving a superior strength-ductility synergy.
main_process_variables: Vacuum arc melting with high-purity raw materials (>99.8 wt %), high vacuum (<1e-6 bar), and high-purity argon protection; repeated melting at least five times
Hot rolling with 40 % thickness reduction, water quenching
Annealing at 900 °C for 5 min, followed by water quenching
Room-temperature tensile testing at strain rate 1e-3 s⁻¹
Quasi-in-situ tensile testing with EBSD and micro-DIC at global strains 0 %, 2 %, 8 %
